sections.heading: S
sections.section_value: The patient reports fatigue for the past two months. The patient reports their sugars are not very well controlled. The patient reports feeling their feet feel like hell.

sections.heading: O
sections.section_value: The patient's blood pressure is 140/60. The patient's eye exams are up-to-date.

sections.heading: A
sections.section_value: The clinician suspects cervical radiculopathy. The clinician notes peripheral neuropathy in the patient's feet.

sections.heading: P
sections.section_value: The clinician plans a cervical MRI. The clinician recommends a medication for peripheral neuropathy if it gets really bad. The clinician wants the patient to increase the glimepiride to four milligrams twice a day. The patient is taking two milligrams of glimepiride twice a day. The patient is on metoprolol 50 milligrams once a day. The patient is on lisinopril with hydrochlorothiazide 20 mg + 25 mg once a day. The patient is on amlodipine 10 milligram once a day. The patient is on atorvastatin. The patient is still smoking.
